title: C2: Communication Topology
description: Link analysis for source-to-destination C2 communications across Sysmon network, VCN Flow, and Network Firewall logs. Use it to pivot from a compromised machine or private IP to the external C2 destination.
query: ((('Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and 'Event ID' = '3' and ('Destination IP' = '198.51.100.77' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*duckdns.org' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*malware.xyz' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*attacker.top' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*cobalt.example.com' or 'Destination Port' = 4443 or 'Destination Port' = 8443)) or (('Log Source' = 'SOC VCN Flow Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Network Firewall Logs') and ('Destination IP' = '198.51.100.77' or 'Threat Name' = 'Suspicious Data Exfiltration'))) | link span = 15minute 'Source IP', 'Destination IP' | eventstats max(Count) as PeakEvents, sum(Count) as Events, distinctcount('Source IP') as Sources, distinctcount('Destination IP') as Destinations

visualizationOptions.tileLayoutXml: <title-text>Peak Path</title-text><title-text>Events</title-text><title-text>Sources</title-text><title-text>Destinations</title-text>

ask_ai_prompts: Trace the C2 path from source IP to destination IP.
Which source IP appears compromised and what evidence supports that conclusion?
